messages.content: Can you shorten the following peragraph "Three sgRNAs targeting the channel catfish mc4r gene in conjunction with Cas9 protein were microinjected in embryos and mutation rate, inheritance, and growth were studied. An overall mutation rate of 33% and 33% homozygosity/bi-allelism was achieved in 2017. Approximately 71% of progeny inherited the mutation. Growth was generally higher in MC4R mutants than controls (CNTRL) at all life stages and in both pond and tank environments. There was a positive relationship between zygosity and growth, with F1 homozygous/bi-allelic mutants reaching market size 30% faster than F1 heterozygotes in earthen ponds (p = 0.022). At the stocker stage (~ 50 g), MC4R × MC4R mutants generated in 2019 were 40% larger than the mean of combined CNTRL × CNTRL families and 54% larger than F1 MC4R × CNTRL mutants indicating mutation may be recessive. With a high mutation rate and inheritance of the mutation as well as improved growth, the use of gene-edited MC4R channel catfish appears to be beneficial for application on commercial farms "?

Three sgRNAs and Cas9 protein were used to target the channel catfish mc4r gene in embryos, resulting in a 33% mutation rate and 33% homozygosity/bi-allelism in 2017. Approximately 71% of offspring inherited the mutation, leading to enhanced growth in MC4R mutants compared to controls at all stages and environments. Zygosity positively influenced growth, with homozygous/bi-allelic mutants reaching market size 30% faster than heterozygotes in earthen ponds (p = 0.022). At the stocker stage (~ 50 g), MC4R × MC4R mutants produced in 2019 were 40% larger than combined CNTRL × CNTRL families and 54% larger than F1 MC4R × CNTRL mutants, suggesting a recessive nature of the mutation. The high mutation rate, inheritance, and improved growth make gene-edited MC4R channel catfish beneficial for commercial farms.

Please make the following pargraph more precise and exciting "Editing genes related to growth achieved promising results of growth. However, in most studies, not data on other economic traits, including feed conversion rate (FCR), meat quality, resilience to diseases, and adaptation of changing environments were not reported. Therefore, it advisable to conduct a series of experiments on these fronts before gene-edited fish are released for commercial production."?

The successful gene editing for growth in fish holds great promise. However, most studies have focused solely on growth and have neglected to report data on crucial economic traits such as feed conversion rate (FCR), meat quality, disease resilience, and adaptability to varying environments. In light of this gap in knowledge, it is imperative to conduct a comprehensive series of experiments that thoroughly examine these aspects before releasing gene-edited fish for commercial production. This will ensure that we have a complete understanding of the potential benefits and implications of gene editing in fish farming, making the future of this technology even more exciting and promising.
What are problems of intermuscular bones in some fish species for human eating?

The presence of intermuscular bones in some fish species can pose several problems for human consumption. Firstly, these bones can be sharp and difficult to detect, making them a choking hazard, especially for children or individuals who are not experienced in eating fish. Accidental ingestion of these bones can lead to discomfort, injury to the throat, or even more serious complications if they become lodged in the digestive tract.

Furthermore, intermuscular bones can also affect the overall eating experience. They can cause inconvenience and frustration by disrupting the smooth texture of the fish fillet, making it less enjoyable to consume. Removing these bones while eating can be time-consuming and may result in the loss of some edible flesh, leading to wastage.

From a commercial and culinary perspective, the presence of intermuscular bones can reduce the market value of fish products. Consumers often prefer boneless fillets for convenience and ease of preparation. Therefore, fish species with a higher incidence of intermuscular bones may have lower demand in the marketplace. This can impact the economics of fishing and aquaculture industries, as well as the availability of certain fish species for human consumption.

To overcome these problems, fish processing techniques such as deboning or filleting methods have been developed to minimize the presence of intermuscular bones in fish products. These methods improve the safety, convenience, and overall eating experience for consumers, ensuring that fish can be enjoyed without the concerns associated with intermuscular bones.

Please rewrite the following paragraph to make it easily understanable "Several candidate genes related to the development of intermuscular bones have been used in gene editing to reduce or remove these small bones in some species. Here, we briefly summarized the outcome of studies and discussed some issues related to remove these small bones in aquaculture."

Gene editing has shown promising results in reducing or eliminating intermuscular bones in certain fish species. Various candidate genes associated with the development of these bones have been utilized in these studies. In this summary, we provide an overview of the findings and address pertinent concerns relating to the removal of these small bones in aquaculture settings.

Can you rewrite the following paragraph to make it easily understandable for a broad aduience "It seems that the results in removing intermuscular bones of knockout of the IMBs are  promising. However, mouse knockout models of various components of the BMP signaling pathway result in embryonic lethality or marked defects [24]. Therefore, it is essential to carefully examine whether runx2b generate any negative effects on economically important traits in aquaculture species. "?

The initial outcomes of using knockout technology to remove intermuscular bones (IMBs) appear to be quite promising. However, it is worth noting that in mouse models, when various parts of the BMP signaling pathway were disrupted, it resulted in either severe deformities or death of the embryos. This indicates the need for caution when considering the potential negative effects of modifying the runx2b gene on economically valuable traits in aquaculture species. It is crucial to conduct thorough evaluations to ensure that any changes made do not unintentionally impact the overall health or desirable characteristics of these fish species in aquaculture settings.
Can you rewrite the following paragraph to make it easily understandable for a broad aduience "Can you rewrite the following paragraph to make it easily understandable for a broad aduience"?

Certainly! Here's a simplified version of the paragraph:

The initial results of using gene editing to remove intermuscular bones (IMBs) in fish species are promising. However, it's important to note that in experiments with mice, disruption of certain genes led to severe defects or even death before birth. Hence, we need to carefully examine whether modifying specific genes in aquaculture species could have any negative effects on important traits for fish farming. It's crucial to ensure that any changes made through gene editing don't unintentionally harm the overall health or desirable characteristics of the fish we rely on for aquaculture.

Can you rewrite the following paragraph to make it easily understandable for a broad aduience? "Meat quality, especially the contents of omega-3 fatty acids, is critically important for human health. Attempts have been made to edit some genes related to fatty acid synthesis to improve omega-3 contents in some fish species. Here, we briefly synthesise the knowledge gained from representative studies. "

The quality of meat, particularly the levels of omega-3 fatty acids, plays a crucial role in human health. Scientists have been working on editing specific genes involved in fatty acid synthesis to enhance the omega-3 content in certain fish species. In this summary, we will provide an overview of the findings from notable studies in this area, helping us understand the progress made in improving the beneficial omega-3 levels in fish meat.

Can you find out the major findings and their significances with 100 words based on the following abstract "The in vivo functions of Atlantic salmon fatty acyl desaturases (fads2), Δ6fads2-a, Δ6fads2-b, Δ6fads2-c and Δ5fads2 in long chain polyunsaturated fatty acid (LC-PUFA) synthesis in salmon and fish in general remains to be elucidated. Here, we investigate in vivo functions and in vivo functional redundancy of salmon fads2 using two CRISPR-mediated partial knockout salmon, Δ6abc/5Mt with mutations in Δ6fads2-a, Δ6fads2-b, Δ6fads2-c and Δ5fads2, and Δ6bcMt with mutations in Δ6fads2-b and Δ6fads2-c. F0 fish displaying high degree of gene editing (50–100%) were fed low LC-PUFA and high LC-PUFA diets, the former containing reduced levels of eicosapentaenoic (20:5n-3) and docosahexaenoic (22:6n-3) acids but higher content of linoleic (18:2n-6) and alpha-linolenic (18:3n-3) acids, and the latter containing high levels of 20:5n-3 and 22:6n-3 but reduced compositions of 18:2n-6 and 18:3n-3. The Δ6abc/5Mt showed reduced 22:6n-3 levels and accumulated Δ6-desaturation substrates (18:2n-6, 18:3n-3) and Δ5-desaturation substrate (20:4n-3), demonstrating impaired 22:6n-3 synthesis compared to wildtypes (WT). Δ6bcMt showed no effect on Δ6-desaturation compared to WT, suggesting Δ6 Fads2-a as having the predominant Δ6-desaturation activity in salmon, at least in the tissues analyzed. Both Δ6abc/5Mt and Δ6bcMt demonstrated significant accumulation of Δ8-desaturation substrates (20:2n-6, 20:3n-3) when fed low LC-PUFA diet. Additionally, Δ6abc/5Mt demonstrated significant upregulation of the lipogenic transcription regulator, sterol regulatory element binding protein-1 (srebp-1) in liver and pyloric caeca under reduced dietary LC-PUFA. Our data suggest a combined effect of endogenous LC-PUFA synthesis and dietary LC-PUFA levels on srebp-1 expression which ultimately affects LC-PUFA synthesis in salmon. Our data also suggest Δ8-desaturation activities for salmon Δ6 Fads2 enzymes "/

The study aimed to investigate the functions and redundancy of Atlantic salmon fatty acyl desaturase (fads2) genes in synthesizing long chain polyunsaturated fatty acids (LC-PUFAs). Two CRISPR-mediated partial knockout salmon were created: Δ6abc/5Mt (mutated in Δ6fads2-a, Δ6fads2-b, Δ6fads2-c, and Δ5fads2) and Δ6bcMt (mutated in Δ6fads2-b and Δ6fads2-c). Fish from both groups were fed diets with varying LC-PUFA compositions. Results showed that Δ6abc/5Mt had impaired synthesis of 22:6n-3, along with accumulation of its precursors, while Δ6bcMt had no significant effect on Δ6-desaturation. Both groups accumulated Δ8-desaturation substrates when fed a low LC-PUFA diet. Furthermore, Δ6abc/5Mt exhibited upregulation of the lipogenic transcription regulator, which implied a relationship between endogenous and dietary LC-PUFA synthesis in salmon. The findings suggest new insights into the functions of salmon fads2 genes and their impact on LC-PUFA synthesis.

Can you find out the major findings and their significances with 80 words based on the following abstract "Atlantic salmon can synthesize polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs), such as eicosapentaenoic acid (20:5n-3), arachidonic acid (20:4n-6) and docosahexaenoic acid (22:6n-3) via activities of very long chain fatty acyl elongases (Elovls) and fatty acyl desaturases (Fads), albeit to a limited degree. Understanding molecular mechanisms of PUFA biosynthesis and regulation is a pre-requisite for sustainable use of vegetable oils in aquafeeds as current sources of fish oils are unable to meet increasing demands for omega-3 PUFAs. By generating CRISPR-mediated elovl2 partial knockout (KO), we have shown that elovl2 is crucial for multi-tissue synthesis of 22:6n-3 in vivo and that endogenously synthesized PUFAs are important for transcriptional regulation of lipogenic genes in Atlantic salmon. The elovl2-KOs showed reduced levels of 22:6n-3 and accumulation of 20:5n-3 and docosapentaenoic acid (22:5n-3) in the liver, brain and white muscle, suggesting inhibition of elongation. Additionally, elovl2-KO salmon showed accumulation of 20:4n-6 in brain and white muscle. The impaired synthesis of 22:6n-3 induced hepatic expression of sterol regulatory element binding protein-1 (srebp-1), fatty acid synthase-b, Δ6fad-a, Δ5fad and elovl5. Our study demonstrates key roles of elovl2 at two penultimate steps of PUFA synthesis in vivo and suggests Srebp-1 as a main regulator of endogenous PUFA synthesis in Atlantic salmon "?

In this study, researchers investigated the molecular mechanisms of polyunsaturated fatty acid (PUFA) synthesis in Atlantic salmon and its regulation. By creating CRISPR-mediated partial knockout of elovl2, a key enzyme in PUFA synthesis, they discovered that elovl2 is crucial for the synthesis of docosahexaenoic acid (22:6n-3) in multiple tissues. The knockout salmon showed reduced levels of 22:6n-3, accumulation of other PUFAs like eicosapentaenoic acid (20:5n-3) and docosapentaenoic acid (22:5n-3), and increased expression of lipid processing genes. These findings shed light on the importance of endogenously synthesized PUFAs and the regulatory role of Srebp-1 in salmon's PUFA synthesis.

Can you find out the major findings and their significances with 100 words based on the following abstract "To make channel catfish an even richer source of nutrients, we produced P1 fish carrying masu salmon (Oncorhynchus masou) elovl2 (OmElovl2) transgene to increase the content of omega-3 (n-3) fatty acids with CRISPR/Cas9-mediated knock-in targeting non-coding region of chromosome 1, and random integration methods. Mosaicism, transgene expression and fatty acids contents were determined. Integration rates of seven-month-old channel catfish generated by CRISPR/Cas9 and random integration methods were 19% and 27.3%, respectively. However, when we tested five tissues including barbel, fin, muscle, liver and kidney of three channel catfish, 13 out of 15 total observations were verified to carry the transgene from three positive P1 fish produced by CRISPR/Cas9 technology. Only five of 15 tissues carrying transgene were detected in three positive P1 fish produced by random integration. Genomic quantitative real-time PCR (qRT-PCR) also suggested that CRISPR/Cas9 transgenic fish had extremely higher average transgene copy numbers than randomly integrated transgenic fish. Additionally, reverse transcription PCR (RT-PCR) and fatty acids analysis revealed that CRISPR/Cas9 P1 fish had strong OmElovl2 transgene expression in most tissues and 20.7% higher DHA than their controls, while randomly integrated P1 fish did not have detectable OmElovl2 expression in any of five tissues detected. There were no significant differences for any fatty acids between transgenic fish produced by random integration and their non-transgenic controls. CRISPR/Cas9 mediated knock-in technology efficiently reduced mosaicism, improved transgene expression and the biological effects of the foreign gene in P1 generation compared to the conventional random integration method. Therefore, transgenesis based on CRISPR/Cas9 technology would shorten breeding programs and improve applications of gene function studies"?

In this study, researchers aimed to enhance the nutritional value of channel catfish by introducing a transgene from masu salmon to increase omega-3 fatty acid content. They utilized CRISPR/Cas9-mediated knock-in targeting a non-coding region of chromosome 1, in comparison to random integration methods. The integration rates of CRISPR/Cas9 and random integration were 19% and 27.3%, respectively. However, the CRISPR/Cas9 approach resulted in higher rates of transgene presence across multiple tissues. Genomic qRT-PCR indicated higher transgene copy numbers in CRISPR/Cas9 transgenic fish. Furthermore, CRISPR/Cas9 P1 fish exhibited stronger transgene expression and 20.7% higher levels of DHA (an omega-3 fatty acid) compared to controls, while random integration did not show similar effects. These findings highlight the efficient and improved outcomes of CRISPR/Cas9 technology in transgenesis, enabling expedited breeding and enhanced gene function studies.
